name: Truman Capote
abstract: Truman Garcia Capote ( kə-POH-tee; born Truman Streckfus Persons; September 30, 1924 – August 25, 1984) was an American novelist, screenwriter, playwright, and actor. Several of his short stories, novels, and plays have been praised as literary classics, and he is regarded as one of the founders of New Journalism, along with Gay Talese, …

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Gourse
about_quotes.evidence: That’s when I think around that time Truman Capote wrote his non-fiction novel, or he was going to write it. It was in the air that non-fiction was going to reign and going to become regarded as high art.
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Gourse interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: I saw, when I was starting to do this Truman Capote was interviewed, the great writer on one of the talk shows.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: There’s a great story about Truman Capote, about that, but I’ll tell you about that another time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Truman Capote once said he felt really lucky that he knew immediately who he was...
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Afterwards, fortunately, Truman called me up and said, “I’m so sorry.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
